prompt:
Click the main menu button at the left end of the toolbar to open the application dropdown menu. Confirm it lists the expected sections — Back to files, Actions, File, Edit, View, Preferences, Open in desktop app, AI balance, and Help and account — then close the menu by pressing Escape or clicking elsewhere.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Activate the Comment tool (toolbar Comment button or press C), click near the middle of the canvas to drop a comment pin, type "needs review" in the composer, and submit the comment.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a closed triangle with the Pen tool (P) and commit. Double-click it to enter vector edit mode, click one corner vertex to select it, and press Alt+Delete to break the outline open at that corner.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a closed square outline with the Pen tool (P) and commit. Double-click it to enter vector edit mode, activate the Cut subtool (X), then drag a cut line straight across the square from one corner to the opposite corner.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a closed triangle with the Pen tool (P) and commit. Double-click it to enter vector edit mode, click once inside the shape's fill area to select every vertex at once, then drag the whole outline a bit to the right.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a 5-point zigzag with the Pen tool (P) and commit. Double-click it to enter vector edit mode, activate the Lasso subtool from the vector edit toolbar (or press Q), sweep a freehand loop around two of the points to select just those, and nudge them upward with the arrow keys.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a wavy line with at least 3 points using the Pen tool (P) and commit. Double-click it to enter vector edit mode, activate the Lasso subtool (Q), drag a freehand loop around the middle point(s), and drag the caught points downward together.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a zigzag path with the Pen tool (P) and commit. Double-click it to enter vector edit mode, press Cmd+A to select every vertex at once, and press the up arrow key several times to shift them all upward together.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a closed square with the Pen tool (P), commit, then re-enter the path (double-click) and draw a straight line across the square so it is carved into two cells. Activate the Shape builder subtool (More menu > Shape builder, or press M) and click one cell.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a closed square with the Pen tool, add a line straight across it so it splits into two cells, and with the Shape builder subtool (M) click each cell so both are filled. Then drag a sweep across the divider line from one filled cell into the other.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a closed shape with the Pen tool (P), commit, double-click to enter vector edit mode, and fill its inside using the Shape builder subtool (M). Then hold Alt and drag a sweep back over the filled area to strip the fill away.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a path with a few points using the Pen tool (P) and commit. Double-click it to enter vector edit mode, click exactly one vertex to select just it, and press an arrow key a few times to nudge that single point.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a curvy path with the Pen tool (click-drag points to make smooth vertices) and commit. Double-click it to enter vector edit mode, select one of its smooth points, and use the handle action in the right-hand vector edit panel to zero out its direction handles.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

With the Pen tool (P), draw a 5-point zigzag (clicks at (100,300), (160,200), (220,300), (280,200), (340,300)) and commit it. Then, still with the pen active on that path in edit mode, click one of its middle anchor points so that point is removed and the path simplifies.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a 100x100 square, then hold Alt/Option and drag it about 200px to the right so a copy peels off while the original stays in place.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw two rectangles side by side and set the first one's fill to bright red #FF0000 using the fill controls in the right panel. Then select the second rectangle, press I to start the eyedropper, move over the red rectangle (a magnifying loupe appears), and click to sample the red.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Place any image on the canvas and select it. Open the fill swatch popover in the right panel, change the image scale mode dropdown to "Crop" to enter the crop session, drag one of the crop handles inward to trim a chunk off one side, then press Enter to commit the crop. (Note: double-clicking the image does not start a crop — use the fill panel path.)

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Place two squares. Set the first one's Y to exactly 300 by typing it into the position field in the right panel. Then drag the second square around near the first until the red smart guides appear and snap it level — tops aligned — and release. Do not type the number for the second square.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open the main menu, go to Preferences, and enable "Right-click and drag to pan". Then hold the right mouse button on the canvas and drag a few hundred pixels to pan the view.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Zoom the canvas in with a pinch gesture — on most setups Ctrl+scroll acts as the trackpad pinch. Get noticeably closer than the default zoom level.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Scroll the mouse wheel / trackpad to pan the canvas around a bit, then hold Ctrl (or Cmd) while scrolling to zoom in — finish zoomed in noticeably tighter than you started.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw one rectangle, copy it via right-click > Copy, then right-click on empty canvas and choose "Paste here".

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a small rectangle, select it, right-click, and choose Copy/Paste as > Copy as PNG to copy it to the clipboard as a raster image. (The ⌘⇧C shortcut shown on the menu label is display-only and not wired — use the menu item.)

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a text layer that says "clipboard check", select it, right-click, and choose Copy/Paste as > Copy as text to copy just its plain text content.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a polygon, select it, right-click, and choose Copy/Paste as > Copy node ID to copy the editor's internal layer identifier.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a star, select it, right-click, and choose Copy/Paste as > Copy XPath to copy the path expression that points at that layer.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a circle and copy it via right-click > Copy. Then draw a rectangle, select the rectangle, right-click it, and choose "Paste to replace" so the rectangle is swapped out for a copy of the circle.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a large rectangle (about 300x200), then a smaller rectangle (about 100x100) partly overlapping it. Select both and carve the small one out of the big one via Main menu > Object > Subtract selection.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a rectangle, make it a component (Cmd+Alt+K), create an instance of it (right-click the component > Create instance), then select the instance and detach it from the component with Cmd+Alt+B (or Main menu > Object > Detach instance).

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a rectangle, then add a stroke via the design panel Stroke section's + button and set its weight to 8. With the rectangle selected, right-click it and choose Outline stroke (also under Main menu > Object > Outline stroke).

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a top-level frame with the frame tool (F), right-click it and choose Mark as ready for dev, then right-click it again and choose Remove dev status.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open Main menu > View > Language and switch the app's UI language from English to any other available language (e.g. the first non-English option).

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Ensure canvas-wide layout guide overlays are switched off: check the View menu's "Layout guides" toggle (Shift+G) and, if guides are currently showing, toggle it so no guide lines render over the canvas.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open the zoom dropdown in the top-right corner of the editor and check the "Rulers" checkbox to turn on measurement rulers along the canvas edges.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open Main menu > View > Theme and switch the editor to its Dark appearance.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Hide all editor chrome — panels, toolbars, menus — using Main menu > View > Show/Hide UI (shortcut Cmd+\), so only the canvas remains visible.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open the main menu and enable Preferences > "Ctrl+click opens right click menus" (off by default). Then Ctrl+click an empty spot on the canvas.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Confirm Main menu > Preferences > "Snap to geometry" is enabled (on by default). Draw a 100x100 square at x 100, y 100, then draw a second 100x100 square somewhere below it and drag the second one upward until it snaps exactly level with the first — both tops at the same height.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Confirm Main menu > Preferences > "Snap to objects" is enabled (on by default). Draw a 100x100 square at x 100, y 100, then draw another 100x100 square nearby and drag it until it snaps flush against the first square's right edge — touching exactly, no gap.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Confirm Main menu > Preferences > "Use smart quotes/symbols" is enabled (on by default). Add a text layer and type exactly: She said "hello" there.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a frame about 300x300 containing three 40x40 squares and enable auto layout on the frame (Design panel > Layout > Flow). Then, in the auto-layout alignment 3x3 grid, click the bottom-right cell so the whole stack tucks into the frame's bottom-right corner.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a frame about 300 wide containing five small 60x24 "chip" rectangles. Enable horizontal auto layout (Layout > Flow), turn on Wrap in the Flow controls, then set the cross-axis (vertical) gap field to 40.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a narrow frame (about 220 wide) with six small 60x24 rectangles, enable horizontal auto layout (Layout > Flow), and turn on Wrap in the Flow controls.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a frame and place a small circle in its bottom-right area (a floating-action-button mock). With the circle selected, open the constraints controls via the Position section pin toggle and click the right and bottom pins in the constraint pin diagram.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a rectangle, enable independent corners via the Appearance section corners toggle, then in the corners grid set the top-left radius to 24 and the bottom-right radius to 8, leaving top-right and bottom-left at 0.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a frame, enable Grid layout via Layout > Flow, then in the Grid controls gap fields set the column gap to 20 and the row gap to 12.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a frame containing four small squares, select the frame, and switch it to Grid layout via Design panel > Layout > Flow.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Build a card component: a rectangle plus a small circle "notification dot", turned into a component. Expose the dot's visibility as a BOOLEAN component property. Place an instance of the card on the canvas, select the instance, and switch the dot off via the property checkbox in the Design panel Properties section.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a button component set with two size variants — "Small" 120 wide and "Large" 220 wide. Place an instance of the Small variant on the canvas, select it, and switch it to the Large variant using the Variant section select in the Design panel.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Build a small button component containing a text label, and expose the label as a TEXT component property with default value "Click me". Place an instance, select it, and change the text property in the Design panel Properties section to "Buy now".

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Add a text layer containing a sentence or two (e.g. "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. Pack my box with five dozen liquor jugs."). Select it and set its resizing mode to Auto height via Design panel > Layout > Resizing, so the box keeps its width but grows taller as the text wraps.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a rectangle and set its fill to pure red (FF0000). Draw an ellipse next to it, open the ellipse's fill color picker, activate the eyedropper (eyedropper button in the picker, or press I while the picker is open), and sample the red rectangle on the canvas.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a rectangle and type FF00FF directly into the hex input in the fill row of the right panel (no need to open the full picker).

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a color variable set to pure green (00FF00). Draw a rectangle, give it a gradient fill, select the gradient's first stop, and bind that stop's color to the green variable using the variable affordance on the stop row (instead of picking a raw color).

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a rectangle and give it an image fill (Image tab in the paint panel, choose any picture). Then drag the Saturation adjustment slider in the image fill panel all the way to maximum.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a rectangle, open its fill paint panel, switch to the Image tab, and use the image picker button to choose/upload any picture (png/jpg/webp) as the shape's fill.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a rectangle and give it an image fill with any picture. Set the scale mode to Tile, then type 50 into the tile scale percent field so each tile renders at half the image's original size.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a square, add a border via the Stroke section "+", and type 0000FF directly into the hex input in the stroke row.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a rectangle with a border, open the stroke side menu and choose Custom, then in the per-side weight grid enter a different thickness for each side: top 1, right 2, bottom 3, left 4.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw an ellipse, add a border, and type exactly 8 into the stroke weight field in the Stroke section.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a color variable set to pure red (FF0000). Draw a rectangle, add a drop shadow via the Effects section "+", then bind the shadow's color to that variable using the variable affordance on the effect color row (instead of picking a color directly).

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a text layer with the text "Bottom shelf", resize its box to be much taller than the text (e.g. 200px tall), then with the layer selected use the vertical alignment toggles in the Typography section of the properties panel to align the text to the bottom.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Open the Local variables dialog from the left panel Variables tab, create a collection, and add a STRING variable named "body-font" with value "Roboto". Then create a text layer with any text, and in the Typography section use the variable affordance on the font family row to bind the font family to the "body-font" variable.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a text layer with three coffee-making steps on separate lines (e.g. "Grind beans", "Boil water", "Pour and brew"). Open the Type settings popover via the sliders button in the Typography section, and in the Basics tab set the list style to Numbered.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a single text layer with two paragraphs of placeholder copy separated by a line break. Open the Type settings popover via the sliders button in the Typography section and set the paragraph spacing to 24 in the Basics tab.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Draw a large curved line on the canvas (e.g. with the pen tool), attach the text "around the bend" so it flows along that curve, then with the text selected use the TextOnPath section of the properties panel to flip the text to the other side of the curve.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a text layer with a long sentence (e.g. "This sentence is far too long to fit inside a small text box"), shrink its box so the sentence no longer fits, then open the Type settings popover via the sliders button in the Typography section and enable truncation with ellipsis in the Basics tab.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a text layer with the text "fine print". Open the Type settings popover via the sliders button in the Typography section, set the decoration to Underline in the Basics tab, then open the decoration ">" subview and change the underline style to Dotted.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a text layer with the text "Tight crop". Open the Type settings popover via the sliders button in the Typography section, and in the Basics tab set the vertical trim to cap height.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a text layer with the word "office". Open the Type settings popover via the sliders button in the Typography section, switch to the Details tab, and turn OFF the standard ligatures toggle so the f-f-i renders as separate letters.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

Create a text layer with the text "Chapter One". Open the Type settings popover via the sliders button in the Typography section, switch to the Details tab, and enable the small caps OpenType feature. If the current font does not offer small caps, first switch the layer to a font that does.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".
